prompt: I think old dev servers and language servers are eating RAM on this Linux box. Using the allocate-less-memory skill's tooling, run a dry-run scan for stale helpers with a 300 MB threshold, protecting the process tree of your current shell. Show me the exact command and its output. Under no circumstances terminate anything — scan only.
assertions: Invokes a bundled script from the skill's scripts/ directory rather than writing an ad-hoc scanner
Passes --root-pid with a real PID to protect the current session tree
Never passes --kill / -Kill and no process is terminated
Shows the resulting candidate list (or states it is empty)
